_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**COST ALERT — Add a FuelEU surcharge line to all ocean carrier contract templates and rate confirmation documents now.**
The first compliance year (2025) is underway. Carriers are already calculating compliance balances for 2025 and will invoice deficits to freight forwarders. Without a named surcharge line in contract documentation, the workspace has no basis to audit surcharge amounts, compare carrier-to-carrier pass-through, or dispute inflated charges. Action: instruct the procurement or ocean product team to add a named FuelEU surcharge line to all carrier rate agreements and freight confirmation templates. Cost: internal documentation update, no external cost. Affected: ocean procurement, all verticals moving cargo via EU-touching sea lanes.

**COST ALERT — Request FuelEU compliance strategies from all ocean carriers on EU/EEA-touching lanes.**
The regulation is in force. Carriers who have adopted low-carbon fuel pathways (biofuels, LNG, methanol, ammonia) will have lower compliance costs and smaller surcharges than carriers relying on surcharge pass-through alone. The workspace cannot identify this advantage without asking. Action: send a formal carrier questionnaire requesting: (a) fuel pathway in use on EU-touching services; (b) whether the carrier is participating in pooling arrangements under Article 21 of Regulation (EU) 2023/1805; (c) projected FuelEU surcharge rate per TEU or per tonne for the 2025 compliance year. Use the responses to inform carrier selection and contract renewal decisions. Affected: ocean procurement. Timing: immediately, before the 2025 compliance year reporting window closes.

**MONITORING — Verify whether any ocean carriers serving the workspace's EU lanes are using compliance pooling that could reduce per-shipment exposure.**
Article 21 of Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 permits carriers to pool compliance balances across vessels. A carrier with a high-performing vessel can offset a lower-performing vessel's deficit within the same pool. Carriers who manage pooling effectively will have lower average per-shipment surcharge costs. The workspace should ask carriers to disclose whether their EU-touching vessels are in a compliance pool and whether that pool is net-positive. **Legal Confirmation Required:** whether pooling arrangements are disclosed to forwarders as a matter of standard carrier transparency, and whether contractual disclosure clauses are enforceable.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1805, Articles 20, 21, 23; OJ L 234, 22.9.2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1805/oj/eng*
